headline: NVIDIA Extends AI Factories Vision With GTC 2026 Agentic And Physical AI
summary: NVIDIA (NasdaqGS:NVDA) used GTC 2026 to launch a broad agentic AI and physical AI platform spanning data center to edge. The company introduced the Vera CPU, Rubin GPU, DSX architecture, and the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform aimed at large scale AI factories. New partnerships include Uber for Level 4 robotaxis targeted for 2027 deployment in 28 cities, plus HPE’s AI Grid and expanded DRIVE Hyperion adoption. NVIDIA unveiled NemoClaw, OpenShell, Nemotron open models, and a physical AI data...

headline: Is Nissan (TSE:7201) Using Tokyo’s Robotaxi Pilot to Quietly Reframe Its Automated Driving Strategy?
summary: Wayve, Uber and Nissan Motor recently signed a memorandum of understanding to develop and pilot autonomous robotaxi services in Tokyo by late 2026, integrating the Wayve AI Driver into Nissan LEAF vehicles operating on Uber’s platform with safety operators initially on board. This collaboration marks Uber’s first autonomous vehicle partnership in Japan and showcases Nissan’s vehicles as a core platform for AI-driven robotaxi deployment in one of the world’s most complex urban markets. Next,...

headline: Nvidia Just Blew a Big Hole in the Case to Buy Tesla Stock
summary: On Monday, at  Nvidia’s  annual GTC conference, CEO Jensen Huang projected $1 trillion in artificial-intelligence infrastructure demand between 2025 and 2027.  It’s an incredible figure that means business for a host of companies, including Nvidia.  The biggest loser in Huang’s scenario, however, might just be  Tesla  with Nvidia’s autonomous driving solutions chipping away at a core part of investors’ reason for owning stock in Elon Musk’s electric-vehicle maker.

raw.headline: Nvidia Just Blew a Big Hole in the Case to Buy Tesla Stock
raw.summary: On Monday, at  Nvidia’s  annual GTC conference, CEO Jensen Huang projected $1 trillion in artificial-intelligence infrastructure demand between 2025 and 2027.  It’s an incredible figure that means business for a host of companies, including Nvidia.  The biggest loser in Huang’s scenario, however, might just be  Tesla  with Nvidia’s autonomous driving solutions chipping away at a core part of investors’ reason for owning stock in Elon Musk’s electric-vehicle maker.

headline: Uber And NVIDIA Expand Robotaxis As Uber Stock Appears Undervalued

The partnership focuses on a fully NVIDIA software-driven robotaxi fleet, using the new NVIDIA Alpamayo AI stack. A phased rollout, including city-specific AI training, targets a global deployment by 2028 as part of Uber's multi-operator robotaxi strategy. headline: Toronto Tech Week Announces 2026 Event Calendar, Homecoming Mainstage Returns
summary: TORONTO, March 17, 2026--Toronto Tech Week, a weeklong, citywide tech initiative, will bring back its mainstage event, Homecoming, on May 27, 2026, at a new venue, History. Following a successful debut last year, Toronto Tech Week is expected to welcome more than 15,000 attendees and host more than 300 events across the city during the weeklong celebration.

headline: Tesla Rivals BYD, Geely To Adopt Nvidia's Self-Driving Tech—Jensen Huang Says 'Everything That Moves' Will Be Autonomous
summary: Nvidia's tech to be used by Chinese automakers BYD, Geely, Isuzu, and Nissan in their self-driving pursuits.

raw.headline: Tesla Rivals BYD, Geely To Adopt Nvidia's Self-Driving Tech—Jensen Huang Says 'Everything That Moves' Will Be Autonomous
raw.summary: Nvidia's tech to be used by Chinese automakers BYD, Geely, Isuzu, and Nissan in their self-driving pursuits.

headline: Nvidia Makes Trillion-Dollar Forecast at Annual Product Expo
summary: During a 2 1/2-hour keynote address, Huang announced plans to push deeper into central processing units — Intel Corp.’s home turf — and introduced semiconductors made with technology acquired from startup Groq.  The company even said it was developing chips for data centers in outer space.  At the heart of Huang’s message: Demand for computing power continues to soar, and Nvidia is uniquely equipped to meet the challenge.
